refresh_on: blast radius or failure class change
materially different recovery or repair candidate
failed remediation or new child incident
independent verifier finds an uncovered scenario
before promotion or closure when source freshness may matter
stop_searching_when: current official evidence and local runtime evidence are sufficient
further access would exceed authority or create observer effects

forbidden_content: raw reasoning or chain of thought

cases.trigger: The current user explicitly identifies a specific occurrence or run as an incident before cause, severity, and scope are known.
cases.expected: Open a bounded incident record, preserve the user report as evidence, state uncertainty, and begin stabilization without debating the label.

cases.trigger: Direct evidence shows user-visible regression, native capability loss, persistence, owner conflict, wrong-object action, or remediation-caused impact without the user using the word incident.
cases.expected: Trip the incident circuit breaker, freeze the suspected path, and create a scoped evidence record.

cases.trigger: An incident remains active while investigation or repair ideas are available.
cases.expected: Stop new work from the suspected automation, limit blast radius, preserve known-good state and evidence, and keep ambiguous objects read-only before remediation.

cases.trigger: A recovery, repair, rollback, external communication, identity action, or control-plane change is proposed during incident response.
cases.expected: Recheck current task authority, object identity, rollback, operation ID, and side-effect scope for that specific mutation.

cases.trigger: Initial classification begins or material evidence, impact, failure class, platform version, candidate action, child-incident state, or verifier finding changes the decision.
cases.expected: Refresh current official primary-source research, record source version and retrieval time, distinguish official fact from local inference, and stop when additional sources no longer affect the decision.
prohibited_effects: external framework installed only to imitate terminology

cases.trigger: A known-good recovery candidate exists and the named effect is authorized.
cases.expected: Select the smallest reversible recovery, verify restoration inputs, execute under one owner, and test the real user path plus prohibited side effects.

cases.trigger: A diagnostic, rollback, recovery, or repair creates a new user-visible impact, capability loss, ownership conflict, or persistence effect.
cases.expected: Freeze the remediation path, link a child incident, preserve its separate timeline and impact, and re-enter stabilization and authority checks.
prohibited_effects: merge child status into parent prose

cases.trigger: The incident is stable enough for causal and process analysis.
cases.expected: Document impact, timeline, trigger, contributing factors, detection, stabilization, recovery, effective and ineffective actions, child incidents, unknowns, and owned corrective actions; obtain independent review.

cases.trigger: The current user explicitly requests a memory update and the incident has a verified lesson supported by external signal.
cases.expected: Store only the bounded lesson fields with provenance, scope, confidence, verification time, supersession or expiry, and an explicit non-authority statement.

cases.trigger: One incident suggests a global contract, memory, skill, router, or eval change.
cases.expected: Require current task authority, supported causal evidence, regression coverage, and independent review before promotion; otherwise keep a candidate action in the postmortem.

cases.trigger: A corrective change claims to prevent recurrence or restore a capability.
cases.expected: Verify the intended result and absence of incident-specific prohibited effects with static checks and proportionate real-environment evidence.
required_evidence: runtime evidence when a runtime claim is made

cases.trigger: Recovery, memory, contract, code, eval, or checkpoint changes have been made.
cases.expected: Check applicable contracts, code/config/hooks, checkpoint, memory disposition, evals, capability claims, live machine state, and unrelated user files for contradiction without broadening scope.

cases.trigger: A static contract, fixture, test label, configuration, or report says PASS while the incident involved runtime behavior.
cases.expected: Keep runtime criteria unverified until the real environment, fresh process, or actual user path supplies matching evidence.
required_evidence: real observation or explicit unverified status

cases.trigger: Some incident objects or recovery targets pass while others remain unresolved.
cases.expected: Report verified, partial, blocked, or unverified separately for the incident task, each affected capability, each child incident, and wider machine or Remote state.
prohibited_effects: language completion merged with machine completion

cases.trigger: The user stops or pauses, changes mode, or reports new harm during research, recovery, repair, verification, or pending memory persistence.
cases.expected: Cancel precisely owned workers and pending writes, freeze new mutations, preserve evidence, and yield an immediate takeover point subject only to the pre-authorized rollback exception.
